title: Array Functions: Sorting
description: Sort arrays with sort, rsort, asort, ksort, etc.

slides.title: Sorting Arrays
slides.content:
# Sorting Functions

PHP has many sorting functions, depending on what you want to sort by and whether to maintain key-value associations.

*   `sort()` – sort indexed array by values (ascending), reindexes keys
*   `rsort()` – sort indexed array by values (descending), reindexes keys
*   `asort()` – sort associative array by values (ascending), maintains keys
*   `arsort()` – sort associative array by values (descending), maintains keys
*   `ksort()` – sort associative array by keys (ascending)
*   `krsort()` – sort associative array by keys (descending)
*   `usort()` – sort using a user-defined comparison function

slides.title: Sorting Associative Arrays
slides.content:
```php
<?php
$ages = ["Alice" => 25, "Bob" => 20, "Charlie" => 30];

asort($ages); // sort by values, keep keys
print_r($ages); // ["Bob"=>20, "Alice"=>25, "Charlie"=>30]

ksort($ages); // sort by keys
print_r($ages); // ["Alice"=>25, "Bob"=>20, "Charlie"=>30]
?>
```

quizData.question: Which function sorts an associative array by values while preserving keys?
quizData.explanation: `asort()` sorts by values and maintains key associations.
